functions.name: easyhid.easyhid._hid_enumerate
documentation:
Enumerates all the hid devices for VID:PID. Returns a list of `HIDDevice`
objects.  If vid is 0, then match any vendor id. Similarly, if pid is 0,
match any product id. If both are zero, enumerate all HID devices.

classes.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.open
documentation: Open the HID device for reading and writing.

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.close
documentation: Closes the hid device

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.write
documentation:
Writes data to the HID device on its endpoint.

Parameters:
    data: data to send on the HID endpoint
    report_id: the report ID to use.

Returns:
    The number of bytes written including the report ID.

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.read
documentation:
Read from the hid device on its endpoint.


Parameters:
    size: number of bytes to read
    timeout: length to wait in milliseconds

Returns:
    The HID report read from the device. The first byte in the result
    will be the report ID if used.

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.is_open
documentation: Check if the HID device is open

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.is_connected
documentation: Checks if the USB device is still connected

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.send_feature_report
documentation:
Send a Feature report to a HID device.

Feature reports are sent over the Control endpoint as a Set_Report
transfer.

Parameters:
    data    The data to send

Returns:
    This function returns the actual number of bytes written

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.get_feature_report
documentation:
Get a feature report from a HID device.

Feature reports are sent over the Control endpoint as a Get_Report
transfer.

Parameters:
    size        The number of bytes to read.
    report_id   The report id to read

Returns:
    They bytes read from the HID report

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.get_error
documentation: Get an error string from the device

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.get_manufacture_string
documentation: Get the manufacturer string of the device from its device descriptor
Probably don't need these excpet for get_indexed_string, since they won't
change from the values found in the enumeration

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.get_product_string
documentation: Get the product string of the device from its device descriptor

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.get_serial_number
documentation: Get the serial number string of the device from its device descriptor

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.get_indexed_string
documentation: Get the string with the given index from the device

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.HIDDevice.description
documentation: Get a string describing the HID descriptor.

A HID device for communication with a HID interface.

Should normally be created through an Enumeration object.

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.Enumeration.__init__
documentation:
Create a USB HID enumeration. The enumeration is a list of all the HID
interfaces connected at the time the object was created.

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.Enumeration.show
documentation: Print the device description of each device in the Enumeration

methods.name: easyhid.easyhid.Enumeration.find
documentation:
Attempts to open a device in this `Enumeration` object. Optional
arguments can be provided to filter the resulting list based on various
parameters of the HID devices.

Args:
    vid: filters by USB Vendor ID
    pid: filters by USB Product ID
    serial: filters by USB serial string (.iSerialNumber)
    interface: filters by interface number (bInterfaceNumber)
    release_number: filters by the USB release number (.bcdDevice)
    manufacturer: filters by USB manufacturer string (.iManufacturer)
    product: filters by USB product string (.iProduct)
    usage: filters by HID usage
    usage_page: filters by HID usage_page
    path: filters by HID API path.
